The left panel has purple (p) cells in a hollow cross pattern:
- Top: rows 51-52, cols 14-15 (2x2 block)
- Left: rows 54-55, cols 12-13 (2x2 block)
- Right: rows 54-55, cols 17-18 (2x2 block)
- Bottom: rows 57-58, cols 14-15 (2x2 block)
The center (rows 53-56, cols 14-17) is gray (g), not purple.

Row 49: gggGGWWWGGggg

Row 58: GGGGGGGGGGGGG

Row 61: gggGGWWWGGggg
```
The grid has white (W) cells in a hollow cross pattern:
- Top: rows 49-51, cols 29-31 (3x3 block)
- Left: rows 54-56, cols 24-26 (3x3 block)
- Right: rows 54-56, cols 33-35 (3x3 block)
- Bottom: rows 59-61, cols 29-31 (3x3 block)
The center (rows 52-53, cols 27-32) is gray (g), not white.
Wait, the grid cells are 3x3 blocks, while the left panel cells are 2x2 blocks. The grid is a larger version of the left panel pattern.
Also, the grid has white (W) cells, and the left panel has purple (p) cells. The colors don't match.
But wait, looking more carefully at the grid:
- The top cross cell is rows 49-51, cols 29-31 (3x3 block of W).
- The left cross cell is rows 54-56, cols 24-26 (3x3 block of W).
- The right cross cell is rows 54-56, cols 33-35 (3x3 block of W).
- The bottom cross cell is rows 59-61, cols 29-31 (3x3 block of W).
- The center is rows 52-53, cols 27-32 (gray g).
And the left panel:
- The top cross cell is rows 51-52, cols 14-15 (2x2 block of p).
- The left cross cell is rows 54-55, cols 12-13 (2x2 block of p).
- The right cross cell is rows 54-55, cols 17-18 (2x2 block of p).
- The bottom cross cell is rows 57-58, cols 14-15 (2x2 block of p).
- The center is rows 53-56, cols 14-17 (gray g).
So both have a hollow cross pattern with the center being gray. The patterns match!
But the colors don't match. The left panel has purple (p) cells, and the grid has white (W) cells.
Hmm, but the left panel also has gray (g) cells around the purple cells. Looking at the left panel:
- Rows 51-52: gggppggg (gray cells around purple)
- Rows 54-55: ppggggpp (purple cells with gray center)
- Rows 57-58: gggppggg (gray cells around purple)
So the left panel has a 9x9 grid (rows 50-58, cols 11-19) with:
- Purple cells in a hollow cross pattern (top, left, right, bottom).
- Gray cells in the center and around the purple cells.
The grid has a 13x13 grid (rows 49-61, cols 24-36) with:
- White cells in a hollow cross pattern (top, left, right, bottom).
- Gray cells in the center and around the white cells.
Wait, but the grid also has dark gray (G) cells. Looking at the grid:
- Rows 49-51: gggGGWWWGGggg (dark gray cells at cols 27-28 and 32-33)
- Rows 54-56: WWWGGgggGGWWW (dark gray cells at cols 27-28 and 33-34)
So the grid has dark gray (G) cells separating the white cross cells. This is similar to the left panel, which has dark gray (G) cells at the border.
Actually, I think the grid and left panel have the same pattern, just with different colors. The left panel has purple (p) cells, and the grid has white (W) cells. The goal might be to make the grid cells purple, not white.
But how do I change the white cells to purple? I've been clicking on cells to change them from gray to light green, and the game over state changes them from light green to white. I haven't found a way to change them to purple.
Wait, maybe the white cells are already "done" and I can't change them. The goal might be to make the remaining gray cells (corners) match the left panel's gray cells. But the corners are already gray, so they match.
Actually, let me reconsider. The left panel has:
- Purple cells in a hollow cross pattern.
- Gray cells in the center and corners.
The grid has:

- Gray cells in the center and corners.
If the goal is to match the pattern (not the colors), then the grid already matches the left panel. But clicking on the blue square at the left end only causes an animation without completing the level.
Maybe I need to change the white cells to purple. Let me try clicking on a white cell to see if it changes color.
Actually wait - I notice the grid has white (W) cells but the left panel has purple (p) cells. Maybe the white cells represent "locked" or "completed" cells that can't be changed. The goal might be to make all the cross cells white (which they already are).
But then why doesn't clicking on the blue square complete the level?
Let me try a different approach. Maybe I need to click on the center cell of the grid to complete the level. The center cell is currently gray, and the left panel's center is also gray. So the center cell matches the left panel.
Actually, I just realized something. The left panel has purple cells in a hollow cross pattern, but the center is gray. The grid has white cells in a hollow cross pattern, and the center is also gray. So the patterns match!
But the colors don't match. The left panel has purple (p) cells, and the grid has white (W) cells.
Wait, maybe the colors don't need to match. Maybe the goal is just to match the pattern (hollow cross with gray center). In that case, the grid already matches the left panel.
Let me try clicking on the right end of the bar (where the cursor is) to submit.
Actually, I just realized that the bar might be a "submit" button. When the cursor is at the right end, clicking on it might submit the grid. Let me try this.
